issue.title: File watcher emits StateChanged on every raw event — no debounce, IPC storms

successCriteria.description: A single logical write to state.json or a plan file produces at most one IPC event to the frontend
Different event keys (state vs plan-N vs release-V) are debounced independently — a fast plan write after a state write is not dropped
Debounce window is short enough (~50ms) that legitimate back-to-back updates from the framework still produce visible UI updates
cargo check passes with no warnings beyond pre-existing

phases.title: Add per-key leading-edge debouncer to the watcher event loop
phases.content:
Add leading-edge debouncing to `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/watcher.rs`. The current event loop emits an IPC `tiki-file-changed` event for every raw notify event, causing 3-5 redundant frontend reads per atomic write.

Design:
1. Add a small free function `debounce_key(file_event: &TikiFileEvent) -> String` that produces a stable key per logical target:
   - `StateChanged` → `"state"`
   - `PlanChanged { issue_number }` → `"plan-{issue_number}"`
   - `ReleaseChanged { version }` → `"release-{version}"`
2. Add a free function `should_emit(last_emissions: &mut HashMap<String, Instant>, file_event: &TikiFileEvent, debounce: Duration) -> bool` that:
   - Looks up the entry by `debounce_key`
   - Returns `false` if the last emission for that key was within `debounce` (caller skips emit)
   - Otherwise updates the map with `Instant::now()` and returns `true`
3. In `start_watcher_internal`, declare `let mut last_emissions: HashMap<String, Instant> = HashMap::new();` and `const DEBOUNCE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(50);` before the recv loop.
4. In the `Ok(Ok(event))` arm, wrap the existing `if let Some(file_event)` block to gate the `app_handle.emit` call on `should_emit(...)`. Add `log::debug!` for the suppressed case so the debounce is observable in logs.

Leading-edge (not trailing-edge) is the right shape: the frontend sees the first signal immediately, and #127's `read_json_resilient` already absorbs the rare race where the first event arrives mid-rename. Trailing-edge would add 50ms to every update.

Imports needed:
- `std::collections::HashMap`
- `std::time::Instant` (Duration is already imported)

The HashMap is bounded by issue/release cardinality (small, finite per project), so no cleanup pass is needed.
verification: watcher.rs declares `last_emissions: HashMap<String, Instant>` and `const DEBOUNCE: Duration` in start_watcher_internal
debounce_key produces distinct keys per StateChanged / PlanChanged / ReleaseChanged
Suppressed events are logged at debug level
